steps.title: Apply the zero product property
steps.content:
The equation $(v - 12)(v - 3) = 0$ is already factored. By the zero product property, when a product equals zero, at least one factor must equal zero:
$$v - 12 = 0 \quad \text{or} \quad v - 3 = 0$$

steps.title: Solve each factor equation
steps.content:
• From $v - 12 = 0$: $v = 12$
  • From $v - 3 = 0$: $v = 3$
The solutions are $3$ and $12$. This matches choice D — D is correct.
